blurb:
Chessie is a cat with a big personality who's had some ups and downs. She came to Good Mews when her previous rescue shut down, spent time in foster care where she immediately established herself as the boss, got adopted for a year, then came back. She's confident, friendly, loves head butts and giving kisses, and gets very excited about wand toys. She'll steal them right out of your hands if she can.

Here's the thing with Chessie: she wants to be the queen cat, so she'd do better as an only cat. And she was returned because she would bite sometimes, unprovoked. Nobody knows exactly why, and it might not happen in a different home, but she needs experienced cat people who understand that some cats have quirks to work through. Not a first-time cat owner situation, and probably not the right fit for young kids.

Chessie is part of Good Mews' Kitty Keeper program, which means her adoption fee is reduced. She's a fantastic cat who deserves someone willing to work with her personality instead of against it. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta.
scoring_notes:
• Multiple placements and returns indicate desperate need for permanent home
• Reduced adoption fee through Kitty Keeper program shows shelter investment
• Complex behavioral history suggests very long shelter stay requiring urgent attention

blurb:
Beignet has had quite the journey. She was adopted from Good Mews as a kitten, but somehow ended up scared and alone at a county shelter. Nobody knows what happened in between, but when the shelter reached out, Good Mews brought her back to safety right away.

She went straight into foster care to rebuild her confidence, and it worked. She started shy but quickly warmed up and showed her sweet side. She loves being petted and is one of those cats who likes company when she eats. When she wants attention, she'll sit close or lean against you. She can give gentle love bites when she gets a little overstimulated, so she needs someone who can read her signals and knows when to give her some space.

Beignet is ready for her second chance at a home where she'll be loved and stay put this time. She's been through enough uncertainty already. Good Mews is a no-kill, cage-free shelter on Robinson Road in Marietta. You'll need to submit an inquiry and visit in person to meet her. Call or email to get started.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking mystery story of abandonment and rescue creates strong reader connection
• Successfully rehabilitated from trauma shows she's ready for loving home
• Extended journey suggests long wait time needing urgent community support

blurb:
Otis was on the county shelter's kill list just for being there at the wrong time. When the rescue team walked up to his kennel, they found him so stressed he was urinating on himself in the overcrowded, loud environment. But the second they got him into a quiet room, everything changed. He buried his head in someone's lap and just stayed there.

Now that he's out of that chaos, Otis has turned into the dog he always was underneath all that fear. He's fun-loving, adores attention, and wants nothing more than to make his people happy. He remembered house-training within days. He remembered how to sit politely and wag his tail. All the things he was too scared to show before.

Whoever adopts Otis is getting a dog who knows what it's like to have nowhere to go, and he's not going to take a good home for granted. The rescue says he'll be loyal and loving for life, and honestly, after what he's been through, that feels like a promise you can count on.

Our Pal's Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find their adoption process and contact information on Petfinder.
scoring_notes:
• Dramatic rescue from kill list creates powerful emotional hook for readers
• Complete transformation story demonstrates his potential as perfect family pet
• Life-threatening situation shows urgent need for immediate adoption

blurb:
Yes, his name is really Pork Tenderloin. No, nobody knows why. But this handsome guy came from a county shelter and he's a little misunderstood. He's sweet, but he likes attention on his own terms. When he's in the mood for affection, he'll come find you. When he's not, he'd prefer you respect that.

Pork Tenderloin gets overwhelmed more quickly than some cats, so he needs a calm environment and someone who won't push. He's not antisocial or aggressive, just particular about his space and his timing. If you understand cat body language and can read his signals, he has the potential to form a really special bond with you.

He needs a cat-savvy adopter who gets that some cats take time and patience. If you're looking for a lap cat who wants attention 24/7, he's not your guy. But if you want a cat who will choose to be with you because he genuinely wants to be there, that's different. Good Mews is on Robinson Road in Marietta. Submit an inquiry and visit to meet him in person.
scoring_notes:
• Hilarious name and mysterious backstory provides newsletter entertainment value
• Honest portrayal of special needs appeals to experienced cat owners
• Particular personality suggests he's been waiting for right match

blurb:
Junior's family suddenly decided they were allergic to him after years of living together. That was their story anyway. So Junior ended up at the county shelter, scared and watching people walk past his kennel day after day. Black dog syndrome is real, and Junior was living it.

Here's what makes Junior special though: this dog has figured out the good life. His favorite thing is ice cream runs to Bruster's and Pelican's. He's got a volunteer friend named Katie who takes him on these outings, and apparently he's the perfect house guest when he stays over. Well-mannered, house-trained, and just genuinely loving to be around.

He does best as the only dog and with older kids, but for the right family, he's going to be the most loyal companion you could ask for. Junior knows what it's like to lose a home once, so when he finds the right one, he's going to appreciate every single day of it.

Our Pal's Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find their adoption process and contact information on Petfinder.
scoring_notes:
• Relatable abandonment story combined with black dog syndrome creates reader sympathy
• Charming ice cream dates and perfect house guest behavior shows adoptability
• Extended shelter stay due to bias factors requires community awareness and action

blurb:
If you want a dog with serious personality, Goober is your guy. He's a chocolate lab who does a spot-on Chewbacca impression and actually sings. Not howling, singing. He's got his own little repertoire apparently.

He's a little cautious when he first meets new people or hears loud noises, but give him a few treats and watch him turn into a social butterfly. Once he's comfortable, he's all about playing with rope toys, giving kisses, and showing off his vocal talents. The rescue calls him goofy, which feels like exactly the right word.

Goober is the kind of dog who's going to make you laugh every single day. If you're looking for a unique companion who brings his own entertainment value, this might be the perfect match. He's ready to find a family who appreciates a dog with a sense of humor.

Our Pal's Place is a rescue in Marietta. You can find their adoption process and contact information on Petfinder.
scoring_notes:
• Unique singing talent and Chewbacca impression provides delightful newsletter content
• Well-socialized personality with minor quirks appeals to broad audience
• Fun-loving chocolate lab breed popularity increases adoption chances significantly
